## ask-for-help.html
Join the society
At Flash Forward, we believe that everyone deserves access to quality energy access, regardless of where they live. If your community or organization is in need of solar energy equipment, training, or other resources to improve energy poverty treatment, we're here to help.
How we can assist?
01
Equipment Donations
We provide state-of-the-art solar energy equipment to communities and organizations in underdeveloped areas. Our goal is to ensure that you have the tools needed to deliver effective energy access.
02
Training and Education
We offer comprehensive training programs for volunteer engineers, including engineers, solar energy specialists, and volunteer engineers. Our training covers the latest techniques and best practices in energy access.
03
Maintenance and Support
Beyond just providing equipment, we ensure that you have the support necessary to maintain and operate it effectively. Our team is available for ongoing assistance and troubleshooting.
04
Advocacy and Partnership
We work closely with local governments, health organizations, and other NGOs to advocate for better energy access infrastructure and policies. We also facilitate partnerships to enhance the overall impact of our efforts.
How to Request Assistance
To request help, please follow these simple steps:
Complete our application form with details about your community or organization, the type of assistance you need, and how it will benefit your recipients.
Thank you!
Your submission has been received!
Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form.

## contact-us.html
Got any questions?
Our team is here to address your inquiries, provide additional information, and assist you.
Thank you!
Your submission has been received!
Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form.

## dedicate-solar.html
A solar light is more than a gift. It’s hope you can hold. Dedicate one in someone’s name, and a student living without electricity receives the light they need to keep learning after dark.
Dedicate a light
Dedicate a solar flashlight in honor or memory of someone, and light the way for a student living without power.
Every light we send carries a name. When you dedicate a flashlight in honor or memory of someone, that light travels to a student who studies by candlelight or in the dark, turning your tribute into hours of learning, safety, and possibility.
Join Our Solar Light Campaign
Dedicate a solar light in honor or memory of someone who matters to you, and send real, lasting light to a student who needs it.
‍
Spark Package
: A personalized certificate in your honoree’s name, plus one solar flashlight delivered to a student living in energy poverty.
‍
Beacon Package
: A framed certificate in your honoree’s name, plus a batch of solar flashlights delivered to students in an off-grid community, with photos of your impact once they arrive.
All donations are tax-deductible and go toward providing solar flashlights to communities without electricity.
‍
Dedicate a light today and spread hope.

## donate-equipment.html
Why Donate Equipment?
Flash Forward's equipment donation program is a vital part of our mission to support underserved communities. When you donate maker and electronics gear, you hand our volunteer engineers the tools they need to build more solar flashlights and get them into the hands of students living without reliable power. Here’s why your equipment donation matters:
01
Direct Impact on Production
Your donation of manufacturing tools and components directly enables our volunteer engineers to produce high-quality solar flashlights at scale, accelerating our ability to distribute light to those in need.
02
Sustainable Energy Independence
By supplying equipment like 3D printers and soldering stations, you help us build a strong, self-sustaining manufacturing pipeline that ensures long-term support for communities facing energy poverty.
03
Give Gear a Second Life
Have a 3D printer, soldering iron, or box of components gathering dust? Donating used-but-working gear keeps it out of a landfill and puts it straight to work building lights for students who need them.
04
Tax Deductible
Your equipment donation is fully tax-deductible, offering you financial benefits while making a profound impact on energy delivery in underserved regions.
How Your Donation Helps
Your equipment donation drives our mission forward in several crucial ways:
More Lights, Made Faster
Tools like 3D printers and soldering stations let our volunteer engineers build solar flashlights at scale, so more students get light sooner.
Hands-On Volunteer Training
Real equipment gives our volunteers a place to learn CAD, 3D printing, and soldering, building practical engineering skills while they build lights.
A Self-Sustaining Workshop
Every donated tool strengthens our workshop, so we can keep producing and improving flashlights for the long haul without buying everything new.
Types of Equipment Needed
We can put a wide range of maker, electronics, and prototyping gear to good use, including but not limited to:
3D Printers & Filament
Soldering Irons & Stations
Solar Panels & Cells
Rechargeable Batteries & Chargers
LEDs, Wiring & Electronic Components
Multimeters & Hand Tools
Laptops for CAD & Design
How to Donate
Donating equipment is easy and impactful.
Follow these steps to make a difference:
Contact Us
Reach out to our team to discuss the equipment you wish to donate.
Arrange Pickup or Drop-off
We’ll coordinate with you to pick up or receive the equipment.
Receive Recognition and Tax Benefits
Your donation will be acknowledged on our website and annual reports, and you’ll receive the necessary documentation for tax purposes.
Join Us Today
By donating equipment, you’re not just giving tools. You’re helping put light in the hands of students who study in the dark. Every tool helps us build more solar flashlights and reach more communities living in energy poverty.
‍
Join us in this mission.
Contact Us
to learn more about donating equipment and start making a difference today.
Thank you!
Your submission has been received!
Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form.

## donate.html
Make a Difference Today
Your donation to Flash Forward directly supports underserved communities with the vital energy poverty and solar lighting equipment they need to save lives. Every contribution, no matter the size, helps provide essential tools and training for volunteer engineers, ensuring better recipient care and improved health outcomes.
Your financial contribution helps us build, deliver, and maintain the solar-powered lights we send to students living without reliable electricity. Every dollar you give is a step toward a brighter future for the kids who receive them.
Together, we can make a tangible difference.
Donate now
and join us in Flash Forward.

## index.html
Provide Solar-Powered Light
to End Energy Poverty
with Flash Forward
We're a nonprofit organization dedicated to bringing solar-powered light to children in off-grid communities across Ghana and beyond.
Upcoming events
Donate now
Join us in our mission to conquer energy poverty by providing solar-powered flashlights to students in need.
Our why and mission
Our story
Flash Forward illuminates education for students living in energy poverty. We design, build, and distribute solar-powered flashlights so children can keep studying after dark.
What started as a small student project has grown into a nonprofit empowering the next generation to create meaningful change.
Thousands of students
Have received solar-powered flashlights to study safely after dark, thanks to our partners, donors, and volunteers.
Join our Society
Support students in energy poverty where it's needed most.
Support students in energy poverty. Donate now to provide them with safe and reliable solar-powered light.
Make a difference.
Fight energy
poverty with us
There are four main ways to help us on our mission. You can volunteer with us, attend our events to show your support, donate to help us improve energy access or volunteer.
By participating in our initiatives and contributing financially, you play a crucial role in expanding access to clean, reliable light. Your involvement and generosity make a significant difference in the lives of those affected by energy poverty.
Donate Equipment
Learn more
Volunteer
Learn more
Upcoming events
Volunteer Adventures
Fundraising Events
See all events
No upcoming adventures yet. Check back soon.
No upcoming fundraising events yet. Check back soon.
Partners
Malaika
Empowers girls and communities in the DR Congo through education, health, and clean water.
COEC Nigeria
Expands access to quality education for children in underserved communities across Nigeria.
Loksadhana
A charitable trust in Maharashtra, India advancing rural education, women’s empowerment, and health.
Rethread Global
A social enterprise creating dignified employment for people rebuilding their lives.
BSREA Bangladesh
Bangladesh’s leading sustainable & renewable energy association, driving clean-energy access.
Brighten Haiti
Brings solar power to schools, hospitals, and families across Haiti via Solar4Schools.
Sponsor-a-Light Project
Follow your sponsored light on its journey to a student in an off-grid community.

## inspiring-stories.html
Contact us
If you have any questions or need further assistance with your application, please do not hesitate to contact us at
support@flashforward.org
or call us at
+1-800-123-4567.
Together, we can spark change and improve energy access worldwide.

## mission.html
Our mission
Flash Forward's mission is to improve energy access, specifically energy poverty relief, around the globe.
Flash Forward provided solar-powered flashlights, training and education to developing countries. We are a 100% volunteer-run, energy-poverty focused nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization.
Brighter
futures
No student should have to stop learning when the sun goes down. Flash Forward is changing the landscape of energy access in developing countries, one solar-powered flashlight at a time.
Our latest achievements
More than 700 million people around the world still live without reliable access to electricity. For students in these communities, learning stops when the sun goes down, leaving only a few daylight hours to study and no safe way to read after dark.
Flash Forward is changing that with durable, solar-powered flashlights. Each light charges during the day and shines for hours at night, so students can keep learning after sunset, with no grid connection, no disposable batteries, and no ongoing cost.
By putting reliable light directly into students’ hands, we help replace unsafe kerosene lamps and candles and give every recipient more time to learn, work, and build a brighter future.
A Case for Support
See where we've helped.
USA (Bellevue, WA)
2017
Fundraising Event
Haiti
Ghana
DR Congo
India
Bangladesh
Our timeline
2024
Flash Forward Foundation was founded by students, developed its first solar-powered flashlight prototype, built its team, established its mission, and began outreach to potential nonprofit partners.
2025
Flash Forward Foundation became an IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) public charity, refined its flashlight design, built partnerships with organizations in multiple countries, launched volunteer programs and workshops, and completed its first international flashlight shipments.
2026
Flash Forward Foundation expanded its global partnerships, continued distributing solar-powered flashlights to students without reliable electricity, grew its volunteer network, pursued nonprofit technology programs and grants, and scaled its organizational infrastructure for larger international impact.

## nonprofit-status.html
Flash Forward Foundation is recognized by the IRS as a 501(c)(3) public charity. Below are our legal and tax-exempt details, along with our official IRS documentation. Donations to Flash Forward Foundation are tax-deductible.
Legal Name
Flash Forward Foundation
EIN
39-3605570
Location
Bellevue, Washington, United States
Tax-Exempt Status
501(c)(3), effective August 5, 2025
Public Charity Classification
509(a)(2)
IRS Publication 78 Listing
Yes, eligible to receive tax-deductible contributions
IRS Determination Letter
IRS Publication 78 Listing
Our first Form 990 covers fiscal year 2025 and will be posted here once filed.

Volunteer
Favorite Hike
North Cascades
My name is Venkata Anirudh, and my favorite place to hike is the North Cascades. I’ve always enjoyed building, tinkering, and figuring out how things work, which is why I find volunteering with Flash Forward Foundation so exciting. Being able to use creativity and hands-on problem solving to make a real difference in students' lives is incredibly rewarding. I’m grateful to be part of a team that combines engineering, innovation, and service to help bring light and opportunity to communities around the world.
Venkata Anirudh Devireddy

## privacy-policy.html
This page explains how Flash Forward Foundation collects, uses, and protects information from visitors to our website, flashforwardfoundation.org.
Updated on July 1, 2026
At Flash Forward Foundation, we take your privacy seriously. Please read this Privacy Policy to learn how we handle your personal information.
By using or accessing our website in any manner, you acknowledge that you accept the practices described below.
Your use of the Flash Forward Foundation website is also subject to our
Terms of Service
, which incorporates this Privacy Policy by reference.
Information We Collect
We collect information you choose to give us, such as your name, email address, phone number, and message when you fill out a form to volunteer, ask for help, donate equipment, or contact us. If you make a donation, our donation platform, GoFundMe, collects your payment and billing information directly — we do not receive or store your card details.
How We Use Your Information
We use the information you provide to respond to your message, process volunteer applications, coordinate equipment donations, keep you updated about our programs, and operate, protect, and improve our website and mission. We do not sell your personal information.
How We Share Your Information
We may share your information with service providers who help us operate our website and programs (such as our form-processing and donation partners), when required by law, subpoena, or legal process, to protect the rights, property, or safety of Flash Forward Foundation or others, or in connection with a merger, reorganization, or transfer of some or all of our assets. We may also share aggregated or de-identified information that cannot reasonably be used to identify you.
Data Retention
We retain personal information for as long as reasonably necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Policy, comply with our legal and tax obligations as a nonprofit organization, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements.
Forms & Third-Party Processors
Contact and volunteer forms on our website are processed by FormSubmit, a third-party service that securely delivers form submissions to our team’s inbox. Donations are processed by GoFundMe. Each of these services has its own privacy policy governing how it handles your data in transit.
Analytics & Cookies
We use analytics tools, including Google Analytics and similar services, to understand how visitors use our website so we can improve it. These tools may use cookies or similar technologies to collect information such as pages visited and general location. You can control cookies through your browser settings.
International Users
Flash Forward Foundation is based in the United States, and any information you provide will be processed and stored in the United States. If you access our website from outside the United States, you consent to the transfer of your information to the United States, which may have data protection laws different from those in your country.
Do Not Track
Some browsers offer a “Do Not Track” signal. Because there is no common industry standard for how to respond, our website does not currently respond differently based on a Do Not Track signal.
Children’s Privacy
Flash Forward Foundation was founded by students, and many of our volunteers are students themselves. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 without parental involvement. If you believe a child has provided us with personal information, please contact us so we can remove it.
Data Security
We seek to protect your personal information from unauthorized access, use, and disclosure using reasonable administrative, technical, and physical security measures. However, no method of transmitting or storing data over the internet is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.
Your Choices
You may contact us at any time to ask what information we have about you, request a correction, or ask us to delete it, to the extent required by applicable law. Depending on where you live, you may have additional rights under applicable data privacy laws; contact us and we will respond as required by law.
Limitation of Liability
While we take reasonable steps to protect your information, to the fullest extent permitted by law, Flash Forward Foundation is not liable for any unauthorized access to or use of your information, or for the privacy or security practices of any third-party service we use or link to, except as required by applicable law.
Changes to This Policy
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time as our website and programs grow. Any changes are effective as soon as we post the updated Policy on this page, and your continued use of our website after a change means you accept the updated Policy.
Contact Us
If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your information, contact us at
about@flashforwardfoundation.org
.

## terms-and-conditions.html
These Terms of Service (“Terms”) are entered into between Flash Forward Foundation (“Flash Forward,” “we,” “us,” or “our”) and any person who accesses or uses our website (together, the “Service”). These Terms explain the rules for accessing and using flashforwardfoundation.org.
Updated on July 1, 2026
Welcome to the Flash Forward Foundation website. Please read on to learn the rules and guidelines that govern your use of our website. If you have any questions, comments, or concerns about these Terms or our website, please contact us at:
about@flashforwardfoundation.org
Address:
Flash Forward Foundation
Bellevue, Washington, United States
These Terms of Service (the “Terms”) are a binding agreement between you and Flash Forward Foundation. By accessing or using our website in any way, you agree to these Terms. These Terms include the provisions in this document as well as those in our
Privacy Policy
.
Who We Are
Flash Forward Foundation is a 501(c)(3) public charity (EIN 39-3605570) founded by students to help end energy poverty. We design, build, and distribute solar-powered flashlights to students living without reliable access to electricity, and we recruit volunteers to help us design, manufacture, fundraise, and ship lights around the world.
Eligibility
You must be at least 13 years old to use our website or submit a form. If you are under 18, you may only use our website and submit forms with the involvement or consent of a parent or guardian. By using our website, you represent that you meet these requirements.
Using Our Website
You may use our website to learn about our mission, dedicate a solar light, donate equipment or funds, apply to volunteer, or contact our team. You agree to use our website only for lawful purposes and in a way that does not infringe the rights of, restrict, or inhibit anyone else’s use of the site.
Donations
Donations made through our website are processed by our third-party donation platform, GoFundMe, and are subject to GoFundMe’s own terms and privacy practices in addition to these Terms. Flash Forward Foundation is recognized by the IRS as a 501(c)(3) public charity, and donations are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. We are not responsible for errors, delays, or issues caused by third-party payment processors.
Volunteer Applications & Contact Forms
When you submit a form on our website — for example, to volunteer, ask for help, donate equipment, or contact us — the information you provide (such as your name, email address, phone number, and message) is sent directly to our team so we can respond to you. Submitting a form does not guarantee a volunteer position, a response time, or any particular outcome.
Volunteering & In-Person Activities
Volunteers are not employees, agents, or legal representatives of Flash Forward Foundation, and nothing in these Terms creates an employment, partnership, or agency relationship. We may accept, decline, pause, or end anyone’s participation as a volunteer at our sole discretion, at any time, for any reason or no reason.
Assumption of Risk
Volunteering, attending events, handling donated equipment, and building, testing, shipping, or using solar-powered flashlights or other hardware associated with Flash Forward Foundation involve inherent risks, including but not limited to physical injury, property damage, and electrical or battery-related hazards. By participating in any Flash Forward Foundation activity or using any product associated with us, you voluntarily assume all such risks to the fullest extent permitted by law.
Indemnification
You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Flash Forward Foundation, its officers, volunteers, and affiliates from and against any claims, damages, losses, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or related to your use of our website, your violation of these Terms, your submission of content, or your participation in any Flash Forward Foundation activity.
No Professional Advice
Nothing on our website constitutes legal, financial, tax, safety, or other professional advice. Any information we provide is for general informational purposes only, and you should seek independent professional advice before relying on it.
Intellectual Property
The Flash Forward Foundation name, logo, and the content on this website (including text, graphics, and photos) belong to Flash Forward Foundation unless otherwise noted, and may not be used without our permission.
If you submit any content to us — including messages, testimonials, photos, or feedback through a form on our website — you grant Flash Forward Foundation a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide, perpetual license to use, reproduce, adapt, publish, and display that content for our mission, programs, and promotional purposes. You represent that you have the right to grant us this license for anything you submit.
Third-Party Links and Services
Our website may link to or use third-party services, including Discord, Instagram, and our donation and form-processing partners. We do not control and are not responsible for the content, policies, availability, or practices of any third-party site or service, and your use of any third-party service is at your own risk and subject to that third party’s own terms.
Disclaimer of Warranties
Our website, its content, and any products, services, or activities associated with Flash Forward Foundation are provided “as is” and “as available,” without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including any implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. We do not warrant that our website will be uninterrupted, secure, accurate, or error-free.
Limitation of Liability
To the fullest extent permitted by law, Flash Forward Foundation and its officers, volunteers, and affiliates will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, or any loss of data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses, arising out of or related to your use of our website or any Flash Forward Foundation activity or product, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages. To the extent any liability is not excluded by law, our total liability to you for any claim arising out of or relating to these Terms or our website will not exceed one hundred dollars ($100).
Termination
We may suspend or terminate your access to our website or your participation as a volunteer at any time, with or without notice, for any reason, including if we believe you have violated these Terms. Sections of these Terms that by their nature should survive termination (including Indemnification, Disclaimer of Warranties, Limitation of Liability, and Governing Law) will survive.
Force Majeure
We will not be liable for any delay or failure to perform resulting from causes outside our reasonable control, including natural disasters, acts of government, labor disputes, internet or utility failures, or disruptions to shipping and supply chains.
Dispute Resolution
If a dispute arises out of or relates to these Terms or our website, we encourage you to first contact us at
about@flashforwardfoundation.org
so we can try to resolve it informally. Any dispute that cannot be resolved informally will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in Washington State, and you consent to personal jurisdiction there.
Changes to These Terms
We may update these Terms from time to time as our website and programs grow. Continued use of our website after any changes means you accept the updated Terms.
Governing Law
These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
Severability, Entire Agreement & No Waiver
If any provision of these Terms is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect. These Terms, together with our
Privacy Policy
, are the entire agreement between you and Flash Forward Foundation regarding your use of our website, and supersede any prior agreements. Our failure to enforce any provision of these Terms is not a waiver of our right to do so later.
Contact Us
If you have any questions about these Terms, please reach out to us at
about@flashforwardfoundation.org
.

## volunteering.html
Join us in making a difference
Flash Forward is on a mission to improve energy access in underserved regions around the world. We believe that by providing equipment, training, and advocacy, we can make a significant impact in the fight against energy poverty. Your volunteer efforts can help us achieve this mission and bring hope to those in need.
Join our Discord
Why volunteer with FlashForward?
01
Real, shipped impact
Every light you build or fund goes to a student who has been studying in the dark. You can watch the number of lights we have shipped climb.
02
Learn on real hardware
Pick up CAD, 3D printing, and soldering next to engineers and other volunteers, on actual flashlights instead of slides.
03
Own your project
Take a design, a campaign, or a grant from start to finish, with your name on the result.
04
Set your own pace
Give an hour a week or lead a whole track. We train you either way, and you decide how much to take on.
Engineering & Manufacturing
Design our flashlights in CAD, then print, solder, and assemble them in the makerspace. Every version should come out cheaper and brighter than the last, and you help get it there. Sketch a single part or own a full redesign; we will teach you the tools either way.
Outreach & Partnerships
Find the companies, schools, and sponsors who can fund lights or help us ship them further. You pick who to contact and how much to take on, from a few warm intros to a full partnership pipeline.
Social Media & Marketing
Make the reels, write the posts, and tell the story behind each light. Bring your own ideas to Instagram and wherever else our audience hangs out. A one-off video counts as much as a full campaign.
Fundraising
Raise the money that pays for lights. That might be federal grant applications, a bake sale, or something nobody has tried yet. You choose which ones to chase.
Volunteer Recruitment
Grow the team. Bring in new volunteers online, on campus, or around town, however you like meeting people. Want to run recruitment your own way? It is yours.
STEM Education
Teach kids near you about engineering and renewable energy through hands-on flashlight builds. We hand you the lesson materials and the parts; you decide when and where to run a session.
Ready to get involved?
Ready to make a difference? Join our community of dedicated volunteers and help us bring hope to those battling energy poverty. Whether you’re a volunteer engineer or someone with a passion for making a difference, there’s a place for you at Flash Forward.
Thank you!
Your submission has been received!
Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form.
